steps.title: Identify the present-time signal
steps.content: "Currently" directly indicates that the painting's location and state at this moment in time are being described.

steps.title: Apply the present-tense rule
steps.content:
Identify the grammar issue: the time marker "Currently" signals a present-moment, ongoing fact. Rule: currently takes simple present tense, not past or future.
  • Choice A — ‘will have hung’: future perfect; describes something completed by a future time, not a present state. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘hangs’: simple present; matches "Currently" — the painting is hanging right now. ✓
  • Choice C — ‘hung’: simple past; contradicts "Currently" by placing the action entirely in the past. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘was hanging’: past progressive; again places the action in the past. ✗
That confirms B is correct.
